heading: Limitations on authorities of the Bureau; preservation of authorities
label: 5517 Limitations on authorities of the Bureau; preservation of authorities

children.heading: Exclusion for merchants, retailers, and other sellers of nonfinancial goods or services
children.label: a Exclusion for merchants, retailers, and other sellers of nonfinancial goods or services

children.heading: Sale or brokerage of nonfinancial good or service
children.label: 1 Sale or brokerage of nonfinancial good or service
children.text: The Bureau may not exercise any rulemaking, supervisory, enforcement or other authority under this title with respect to a person who is a merchant, retailer, or seller of any nonfinancial good or service and is engaged in the sale or brokerage of such nonfinancial good or service, except to the extent that such person is engaged in offering or providing any consumer financial product or service, or is otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Offering or provision of certain consumer financial products or services in connection with the sale or brokerage of nonfinancial good or service
children.label: 2 Offering or provision of certain consumer financial products or services in connection with the sale or brokerage of nonfinancial good or service
i
children.text: extends credit directly to a consumer, in a case in which the good or service being provided is not itself a consumer financial product or service (other than credit described in this subparagraph), exclusively for the purpose of enabling that consumer to purchase such nonfinancial good or service directly from the merchant, retailer, or seller;

children.label: ii
children.text: directly, or through an agreement with another person, collects debt arising from credit extended as described in clause (i); or

children.label: iii
children.text: sells or conveys debt described in clause (i) that is delinquent or otherwise in default.

children.label: i
children.text: in which the merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services assigns, sells or otherwise conveys to another person such debt owed by the consumer (except for a sale of debt that is delinquent or otherwise in default, as described in subparagraph (A)(iii));

children.label: ii
children.text: in which the credit extended significantly exceeds the market value of the nonfinancial good or service provided, or the Bureau otherwise finds that the sale of the nonfinancial good or service is done as a subterfuge, so as to evade or circumvent the provisions of this title; or1

children.label: iii
children.text: in which the merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services regularly extends credit and the credit is subject to a finance charge.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (B), subparagraph (A) shall apply with respect to a merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services that is not engaged significantly in offering or providing consumer financial products or services.

children.label: I
children.text: if such merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services is engaged in a transaction described in subparagraph (B)(i) or (B)(ii); or

children.label: II
children.text: to the extent that such merchant, retailer, or seller is subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H, but the Bureau may exercise such authority only with respect to that law.

children.heading: Authority of other agencies
children.label: i Authority of other agencies
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as modifying, limiting, or superseding the supervisory or enforcement authority of the Federal Trade Commission or any other agency (other than the Bureau) with respect to credit extended, or the collection of debt arising from such extension, directly by a merchant or retailer to a consumer exclusively for the purpose of enabling that consumer to purchase nonfinancial goods or services directly from the merchant or retailer.1

children.label: I
children.text: only extends credit for the sale of nonfinancial goods or services, as described in subparagraph (A)(i);

children.label: II
children.text: retains such credit on its own accounts (except to sell or convey such debt that is delinquent or otherwise in default); and

children.label: III
children.text: meets the relevant industry size threshold to be a small business concern, based on annual receipts, pursuant to section 3 of the Small Business Act () and the implementing rules thereunder.15 U.S.C. 632

children.heading: Initial year
children.label: iii Initial year
children.text: A merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services shall be deemed to meet the relevant industry size threshold described in clause (ii)(III) during the first year of operations of that business concern if, during that year, the receipts of that business concern reasonably are expected to meet that size threshold.

children.heading: Other standards for small business
children.label: iv Other standards for small business
children.text: With respect to a merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services that is a classified on a basis other than annual receipts for the purposes of section 3 of the Small Business Act () and the implementing rules thereunder, such merchant, retailer, or seller shall be deemed to meet the relevant industry size threshold described in clause (ii)(III) if such merchant, retailer, or seller meets the relevant industry size threshold to be a small business concern based on the number of employees, or other such applicable measure, established under that Act [ et seq.].15 U.S.C. 63215 U.S.C. 631

children.heading: Exception from State enforcement
children.label: E Exception from State enforcement
children.text: To the extent that the Bureau may not exercise authority under this subsection with respect to a merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services, no action by a State attorney general or State regulator with respect to a claim made under this title may be brought under sub, with respect to an activity described in any of clauses (i) through (iii) of subparagraph (A) by such merchant, retailer, or seller of nonfinancial goods or services.1section 5552(a) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion for real estate brokerage activities
children.label: b Exclusion for real estate brokerage activities
A
children.text: acts as a real estate agent or broker for a buyer, seller, lessor, or lessee of real property;

children.label: B
children.text: brings together parties interested in the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange of real property;

children.label: C
children.text: negotiates, on behalf of any party, any portion of a contract relating to the sale, purchase, lease, rental, or exchange of real property (other than in connection with the provision of financing with respect to any such transaction); or

children.label: D
children.text: offers to engage in any activity, or act in any capacity, described in subparagraph (A), (B), or (C).

children.label: A
children.text: engaged in an activity of offering or providing any consumer financial product or service, except that the Bureau may exercise such authority only with respect to that activity; or

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H, but the Bureau may exercise such authority only with respect to that law.

children.heading: Exclusion for manufactured home retailers and modular home retailers
children.label: c Exclusion for manufactured home retailers and modular home retailers
A
children.text: such person is not described in paragraph (2); and

children.label: i
children.text: acts as an agent or broker for a buyer or seller of a manufactured home or a modular home;

children.label: ii
children.text: facilitates the purchase by a consumer of a manufactured home or modular home, by negotiating the purchase price or terms of the sales contract (other than providing financing with respect to such transaction); or

children.label: iii
children.text: offers to engage in any activity described in clause (i) or (ii).

children.heading: Description of activities
children.label: 2 Description of activities
children.text: A person is described in this paragraph to the extent that such person is engaged in the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service or is otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: Manufactured home
children.label: A Manufactured home
children.text: The term “manufactured home” has the same meaning as in .section 5402 of title 42

children.heading: Modular home
children.label: B Modular home
children.text: The term “modular home” means a house built in a factory in 2 or more modules that meet the State or local building codes where the house will be located, and where such modules are transported to the building site, installed on foundations, and completed.

children.heading: Exclusion for accountants and tax preparers
children.label: d Exclusion for accountants and tax preparers
i
children.text: customary and usual accounting activities, including the provision of accounting, tax, advisory, or other services that are subject to the regulatory authority of a State board of accountancy or a Federal authority; or

children.label: I
children.text: by the person separate and apart from such customary and usual accounting activities; or

children.label: II
children.text: to consumers who are not receiving such customary and usual accounting activities; or

children.label: B
children.text: any person, other than a person described in subparagraph (A) that performs income tax preparation activities for consumers.2So in original. Probably should be followed by a comma.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any person described in paragraph (1)(A) or (1)(B) to the extent that such person is engaged in any activity which is not a customary and usual accounting activity described in paragraph (1)(A) or incidental thereto but which is the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service, except to the extent that a person described in paragraph (1)(A) is engaged in an activity which is a customary and usual accounting activity described in paragraph (1)(A), or incidental thereto.

children.heading: Not a customary and usual accounting activity
children.label: B Not a customary and usual accounting activity
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, extending or brokering credit is not a customary and usual accounting activity, or incidental thereto.

children.label: i
children.text: not subject to a finance charge; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not payable by written agreement in more than 4 installments.

children.heading: Other limitations
children.label: D Other limitations
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to any person described in paragraph (1)(A) or (1)(B) that is otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: Exclusion for practice of law
children.label: e Exclusion for practice of law

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided under paragraph (2), the Bureau may not exercise any supervisory or enforcement authority with respect to an activity engaged in by an attorney as part of the practice of law under the laws of a State in which the attorney is licensed to practice law.

children.label: A
children.text: that is not offered or provided as part of, or incidental to, the practice of law, occurring exclusively within the scope of the attorney-client relationship; or

children.label: B
children.text: that is otherwise offered or provided by the attorney in question with respect to any consumer who is not receiving legal advice or services from the attorney in connection with such financial product or service.

children.heading: Existing authority
children.label: 3 Existing authority
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not be construed so as to limit the authority of the Bureau with respect to any attorney, to the extent that such attorney is otherwise subject to any of the enumerated consumer laws or the authorities transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: Exclusion for persons regulated by a State insurance regulator
children.label: f Exclusion for persons regulated by a State insurance regulator

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of any State insurance regulator to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by a State insurance regulator. Except as provided in paragraph (2), the Bureau shall have no authority to exercise any power to enforce this title with respect to a person regulated by a State insurance regulator.11

children.heading: Description of activities
children.label: 2 Description of activities
children.text: Paragraph (1) does not apply to any person described in such paragraph to the extent that such person is engaged in the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service or is otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: State insurance authority under Gramm-Leach-Bliley
children.label: 3 State insurance authority under Gramm-Leach-Bliley
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (2), the Bureau shall not exercise any authorities that are granted a State insurance authority under with respect to a person regulated by a State insurance authority.section 6805(a)(6) of title 15

children.heading: Exclusion for employee benefit and compensation plans and certain other arrangements under title 26
children.label: g Exclusion for employee benefit and compensation plans and certain other arrangements under title 26

children.heading: Preservation of authority of other agencies
children.label: 1 Preservation of authority of other agencies
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of the Secretary of the Treasury, the Secretary of Labor, or the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to adopt regulations, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any actions with respect to any specified plan or arrangement.1

children.heading: Activities not constituting the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service
children.label: 2 Activities not constituting the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service
A
children.text: a specified plan or arrangement;

children.label: B
children.text: engaged in the activity of establishing or maintaining, for the benefit of employees of such person (or for members of an employee organization), any specified plan or arrangement; or

children.label: C
children.text: engaged in the activity of establishing or maintaining a qualified tuition program under offered by a State or other prepaid tuition program offered by a State.section 529(b)(1) of title 26

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided under subparagraphs (B) and (C), the Bureau may not exercise any rulemaking or enforcement authority with respect to products or services that relate to any specified plan or arrangement.

children.heading: Bureau action pursuant to agency request
children.label: B Bureau action pursuant to agency request

children.heading: Agency request
children.label: i Agency request
children.text: The Secretary and the Secretary of Labor may jointly issue a written request to the Bureau regarding implementation of appropriate consumer protection standards under this title with respect to the provision of services relating to any specified plan or arrangement.1

children.heading: Agency response
children.label: ii Agency response
children.text: In response to a request by the Bureau, the Secretary and the Secretary of Labor shall jointly issue a written response, not later than 90 days after receipt of such request, to grant or deny the request of the Bureau regarding implementation of appropriate consumer protection standards under this title with respect to the provision of services relating to any specified plan or arrangement.1

children.heading: Scope of Bureau action
children.label: iii Scope of Bureau action
children.text: Subject to a request or response pursuant to clause (i) or clause (ii) by the agencies made under this subparagraph, the Bureau may exercise rulemaking authority, and may act to enforce a rule prescribed pursuant to such request or response, in accordance with the provisions of this title. A request or response made by the Secretary and the Secretary of Labor under this subparagraph shall describe the basis for, and scope of, appropriate consumer protection standards to be implemented under this title with respect to the provision of services relating to any specified plan or arrangement.11

children.heading: Description of products or services
children.label: C Description of products or services
children.text: To the extent that a person engaged in providing products or services relating to any specified plan or arrangement is subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H, subparagraph (A) shall not apply with respect to that law.

children.heading: Specified plan or arrangement
children.label: 4 Specified plan or arrangement
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “specified plan or arrangement” means any plan, account, or arrangement described in section 220, 223, 401(a), 403(a), 403(b), 408, 408A, 529, 529A, or 530 of title 26, or any employee benefit or compensation plan or arrangement, including a plan that is subject to title I of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [ et seq.], or any prepaid tuition program offered by a State.29 U.S.C. 1001

children.heading: Persons regulated by a State securities commission
children.label: h Persons regulated by a State securities commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of any securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of any State to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by any securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of any State. Except as permitted in paragraph (2) and subsection (f), the Bureau shall have no authority to exercise any power to enforce this title with respect to a person regulated by any securities commission (or any agency or office performing like functions) of any State, but only to the extent that the person acts in such regulated capacity.11

children.heading: Description of activities
children.label: 2 Description of activities
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to any person to the extent such person is engaged in the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service, or is otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: Exclusion for persons regulated by the Commission
children.label: i Exclusion for persons regulated by the Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No provision of this title may be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of the Commission to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by the Commission. The Bureau shall have no authority to exercise any power to enforce this title with respect to a person regulated by the Commission.11

children.heading: Consultation and coordination
children.label: 2 Consultation and coordination
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the Commission shall consult and coordinate, where feasible, with the Bureau with respect to any rule (including any advance notice of proposed rulemaking) regarding an investment product or service that is the same type of product as, or that competes directly with, a consumer financial product or service that is subject to the jurisdiction of the Bureau under this title or under any other law. In carrying out this paragraph, the agencies shall negotiate an agreement to establish procedures for such coordination, including procedures for providing advance notice to the Bureau when the Commission is initiating a rulemaking.1

children.heading: Exclusion for persons regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission
children.label: j Exclusion for persons regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of the Commodity Futures Trading Commission to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission. The Bureau shall have no authority to exercise any power to enforce this title with respect to a person regulated by the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.11

children.heading: Consultation and coordination
children.label: 2 Consultation and coordination
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1), the Commodity Futures Trading Commission shall consult and coordinate with the Bureau with respect to any rule (including any advance notice of proposed rulemaking) regarding a product or service that is the same type of product as, or that competes directly with, a consumer financial product or service that is subject to the jurisdiction of the Bureau under this title or under any other law.1

children.heading: Exclusion for persons regulated by the Farm Credit Administration
children.label: k Exclusion for persons regulated by the Farm Credit Administration

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as altering, amending, or affecting the authority of the Farm Credit Administration to adopt rules, initiate enforcement proceedings, or take any other action with respect to a person regulated by the Farm Credit Administration. The Bureau shall have no authority to exercise any power to enforce this title with respect to a person regulated by the Farm Credit Administration.11

children.heading: Definition
children.label: 2 Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “person regulated by the Farm Credit Administration” means any Farm Credit System institution that is chartered and subject to the provisions of the Farm Credit Act of 1971 ( et seq.).12 U.S.C. 2001

children.heading: Exclusion for activities relating to charitable contributions
children.label: l Exclusion for activities relating to charitable contributions

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director and the Bureau may not exercise any rulemaking, supervisory, enforcement, or other authority, including authority to order penalties, over any activities related to the solicitation or making of voluntary contributions to a tax-exempt organization as recognized by the Internal Revenue Service, by any agent, volunteer, or representative of such organizations to the extent the organization, agent, volunteer, or representative thereof is soliciting or providing advice, information, education, or instruction to any donor or potential donor relating to a contribution to the organization.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The exclusion in paragraph (1) does not apply to other activities not described in paragraph (1) that are the offering or provision of any consumer financial product or service, or are otherwise subject to any enumerated consumer law or any law for which authorities are transferred under subtitle F or H.

children.heading: Insurance
children.label: m Insurance
children.text: The Bureau may not define as a financial product or service, by regulation or otherwise, engaging in the business of insurance.

children.heading: Limited authority of the Bureau
children.label: n Limited authority of the Bureau

children.text: may be a service provider; and

may be subject to requests from, or requirements imposed by, the Bureau regarding information in order to carry out the responsibilities and functions of the Bureau and in accordance with section 5512, 5562, or 5563 of this title.

children.heading: No authority to impose usury limit
children.label: o No authority to impose usury limit
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as conferring authority on the Bureau to establish a usury limit applicable to an extension of credit offered or made by a covered person to a consumer, unless explicitly authorized by law.1

children.heading: Attorney General
children.label: p Attorney General
children.text: No provision of this title, including , shall affect the authorities of the Attorney General under otherwise applicable provisions of law.1section 5514(c)(1) of this title

children.heading: Secretary of the Treasury
children.label: q Secretary of the Treasury
children.text: No provision of this title shall affect the authorities of the Secretary, including with respect to prescribing rules, initiating enforcement proceedings, or taking other actions with respect to a person that performs income tax preparation activities for consumers.1

children.heading: Deposit insurance and share insurance
children.label: r Deposit insurance and share insurance
children.text: Nothing in this title shall affect the authority of the Corporation under the Federal Deposit Insurance Act [ et seq.] or the National Credit Union Administration Board under the Federal Credit Union Act [ et seq.] as to matters related to deposit insurance and share insurance, respectively.112 U.S.C. 181112 U.S.C. 1751

children.heading: Fair Housing Act
children.label: s Fair Housing Act
children.text: No provision of this title shall be construed as affecting any authority arising under the Fair Housing Act [ et seq.].142 U.S.C. 3601
